mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 200k persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations upward through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns when PAYEMS growth momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 payroll level 141.8M persons indicates continued labor market tightening
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth months do not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative growth months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.50% after an extended near-zero period, it signals the Fed's confidence in economic recovery and the beginning of a tightening cycle. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns as equity risk premiums expand and discount rates rise, compressing valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 FEDFUNDS at 0.13% remains in accommodation zone
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.50%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, it indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days. This interaction-driven mechanism requires both proxies to confirm the signal.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS show positive month-over-month growth in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:May 2015 HOUST at 1.036M units and RSAFS at 442B dollars both elevated
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint positive growth months do not produce significantly higher 60-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. When INDPRO falls more than 1% month-over-month, it triggers a negative signal for 5-day forward returns as equity investors reprrice earnings expectations downward and demand inflation hedges.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative INDPRO signal, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year in a low-rate environment (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates policy uncertainty and signals potential future rate hikes. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns as equity investors demand higher risk premiums and expect monetary tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2015 CPI at 237.0 index, FEDFUNDS at 0.13%
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or triggered instances do not produce significantly lower 20-day returns than non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.30 percentage points in a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 60-day forward returns as equity investors anticipate earnings downgrades and reduced consumer spending.
evidence_refs: fact:June 2015 UNRATE at 5.3% indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly serves as an always-on trend signal. Quarters with positive real GDP growth relative to the prior quarter predict positive 60-day forward returns as equity investors extrapolate economic expansion and corporate earnings growth.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth quarters do not produce significantly higher 60-day forward returns than negative growth quarters (t-test p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) diverge when RSAFS grows but HOUST contracts, signaling consumer strength masking construction weakness. This regime-conditional interaction mechanism predicts positive 5-day returns in early-cycle expansions but negative returns in late-cycle periods when divergence signals demand reallocation away from investment.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth < -0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:May 2015 RSAFS at 442B and HOUST at 1.036M units
regime_hint: demand composition shift in mid-cycle expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC of the divergence interaction < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or triggered instances do not produce significantly different 5-day returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.12).
